title: Campium argutum
taxonomy: Campium argutum is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Campium. Further classification includes the family and order, Thelypteridaceae and Thelypterales, respectively. The class and division of Campium argutum are Polypodiopsida and Pteridophyta, respectively. This plant is native to Asia.
constituents: Campium argutum contains active constituents that may include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds are often found in plants and may contribute to the medicinal properties of Campium argutum. The flavonoids in the plant may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The phenolic acids could help protect the plant from oxidative stress and pathogens. More research is needed to fully understand the properties and effects of these active constituents in Campium argutum.
